growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Growth yield (OD600) 0.79±0.01; Specific growth rate (h-1) 0.58±0.19 ... Fumarate (mM) -5.5, Succinate (mM) +2.2, Acetate (mM) +21.8, Ethanol (mM) +8.7 [pure culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu=0.58 h-1) directly reported in Table 1 of paper (pure culture of R. albus 7, anaerobic modified Balch medium, headspace 20% CO2/80% N2, 37 degC). Uptake/secretion rates are CALCULATED as delta-concentration (Table 1 footnote: change between 0 and 12 h) divided by 12 h; these are volumetric (mM/h) not biomass-specific rates because the paper gives no OD-to-gDW conversion factor for R. albus. Fumarate: 5.5 mM/12h=0.458 mM/h; succinate: 2.2/12=0.183; acetate: 21.8/12=1.817; ethanol: 8.7/12=0.725 mM/h. Headspace H2 also increased +5.3% over 12h (gas-phase %, not concentration, so not converted to a rate here). Medium composition (Table S6) not reproduced in main text.

growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.medium: modified Balch medium (co-culture with Wolinella succinogenes DSM 1740)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Specific growth rate (h-1) co-culture 0.57±0.34 (Ra)/0.54±0.11 (Ws) ... Fumarate (mM) -43.1, Succinate +55.4, Acetate +32.4, Ethanol +0.3, H2 BDL [co-culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: R. albus growth rate in co-culture (mu=0.57 h-1) directly reported in Table 1, tracked by qPCR alongside W. succinogenes. Metabolite concentration changes (Table 1, 0-12h) are for the COMBINED co-culture and cannot be attributed solely to R. albus; rates calculated as delta-conc/12h: fumarate 43.1/12=3.592, succinate 55.4/12=4.617, acetate 32.4/12=2.700, ethanol 0.3/12=0.025 mM/h. H2 was below detection limit in co-culture (fully consumed). Same combined-culture metabolite rates also attached to the paired W. succinogenes co-culture record; volumetric (mM/h), not gDW-specific.
